issuesFound.title: * `faqAccordion.open` - Missing `aria-hidden` removal on panel.
* `faqAccordion.close` - Missing `aria-hidden` addition on panel.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions toggle CSS classes and scroll locks but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the hamburger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1542: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`, Line 1565: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period buttons (Monthly/Annual) update visual active states but do not reflect the selection via aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify the selected billing cycle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2454: `btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch (month/year) is assigned role="switch" but its checked state is never updated programmatically. Users of assistive technology will always hear the initial state regardless of interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', String(next === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the result count badge is updated visually, but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users must manually search the page to find how many items matched their filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2325: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) + ": " + totalMatches + " products found");`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and error messages but fails to set the aria-invalid="true" attribute. Screen readers will not identify the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2865: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The clearField function resets the visual error state but does not remove or update the aria-invalid attribute. A field previously marked invalid will continue to be announced as such even after being corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2900: `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn transform
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons use transform transitions (translateY) for hover and active states without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unnecessary movement.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 410: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:hover, .btn:active { transform: none; transition-duration: 0s; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img transform

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller shows/hides panels but does not set aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Screen reader users won't know the expanded state of dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance function, add: trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); In _closeInstance function, add: trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion expands/collapses answer panels but does not update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Screen reader users cannot track which questions are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open function, add: item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); In faqAccordion.close function, add: item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) change the active state but do not set aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod function, add: periodButtons.forEach(btn => btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period ? 'true' : 'false'));

issuesFound.title: Product filter application does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count and visibility change dynamically, but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the updated results.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge in applyFilters, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion updates do not announce via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When search suggestions are fetched and displayed, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not notified that suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering suggestions in _fetchAndRender, add: liveRegion.announce(`${results.length} suggestions available`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched, price displays update dynamically but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod function, add: liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked invalid, CSS classes are added but aria-invalid is not set. Screen reader users cannot programmatically detect invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult function, when result.valid is false, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); when valid, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized when the page loads. Screen reader users start with an unknown state for the menu.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init function, add: trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded initialized when the page loads. Screen reader users start with unknown expanded states for dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.init function, after registering instances, add: trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter animation does not set aria-hidden on hidden cards
issuesFound.explanation: When product cards are hidden by filters, they are visually hidden but remain in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users may still encounter hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In filterAnimation.run function, in the toHide loop, add: el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); in the toShow loop, add: el.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion panel close does not set aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the search suggestion panel is closed, it is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users may still encounter hidden suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: In siteSearch._close function, add: suggestionPanel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not set aria-hidden on answer panel
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ answer is collapsed, the panel is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users may still encounter hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.close function, add: item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); in open function, add: item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');

fpTitles: Dropdown menu triggers do not initialize aria-expanded on page load

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions change the visible state of the drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the open function, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); In the close function, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu openInstance/closeInstance do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions toggle the panel visibility without updating aria-expanded on the associated trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); In _closeInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (month/year) change the active state visually but do not set aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which option is selected.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncToggleState, for each button in _periodBtns, add: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, btnPeriod === period);

issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, there is no live region announcement of the result count. Screen reader users are not informed of the dynamic content change.
issuesFound.fix: After _updateCountBadge(totalMatches), add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) : `${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav init, after _trigger is assigned, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1056: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1074: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up event listeners for the navigation toggle but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state. This leaves the trigger's state ambiguous until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1138: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
Dropdown‑menu trigger does not update **aria‑expanded**
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `openInstance` and `closeInstance` (lines 1152‑1158) add/remove CSS classes to show/hide a dropdown panel, but they never set or clear `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button (`SELECTORS.navDropdownTrigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update **aria‑pressed**
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period switch (`[data-billing-toggle]`) is treated as a toggle (on/off) but the code never updates `aria‑pressed` (see `pricing._syncToggleState` and the click/keydown handlers). Users of assistive tech receive no state feedback.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field does **not** set **aria‑invalid** on error
Contact form fields do **not** set **aria‑invalid** on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The same `inlineValidator` is used for the contact form. Because `aria‑invalid` is never set, screen‑reader users cannot detect which fields are invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same change as in Issue 4 (the fix is global to `inlineValidator` and will cover all forms).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger missing initial **aria‑expanded="false"**
issuesFound.explanation:
On page load the dropdown buttons have `aria-haspopup` but no `aria‑expanded` attribute, violating the requirement that the collapsed state be expressed in markup.  
  Fix (add during registration):  
  ```javascript
  function register(trigger, panel) {
    …
    setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← add
    …
  }
  ```

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial **aria‑expanded="false"**
issuesFound.explanation:
FAQ question buttons are created without an initial `aria‑expanded` value, so the collapsed state is not conveyed to AT.  
  Fix (add in `faqRegistry.parse`):  
  ```javascript
  setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← add
  ```

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation drawer hidden via CSS but not marked with **aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation:
The drawer (`SELECTORS.navMenu`) is shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes (`CSS.open`) and by toggling `document.body` classes, but the code never adds `aria‑hidden="true"` when the drawer is closed nor removes it when opened. AT will still treat the drawer as visible.  
  Fix (in `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close`):  
  ```javascript
  function open() {
    …
    setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); // ← add
    …
  }
  function close() {
    …
    setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); // ← add
    …
  }
  ```

issuesFound.title: Changing billing period does **not** announce the change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly/quarterly/annual billing, the UI updates prices but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving AT users unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a FAQ item updates the UI but does not provide any audible feedback. AT users cannot know that an answer was revealed or hidden.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced**
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the numeric badge showing the number of matching products is updated, but no live‑region is used, so AT users receive no feedback about the new result count.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a live‑region announcement after updating the badge:  
  ```javascript
  function _updateCountBadge(count) {
    …
    liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} found`); // ← add
  }
  ```

issuesFound.title: Switching product view mode (grid ↔ list) is not announced**
issuesFound.explanation: The user can toggle between grid and list layouts, but the change is not communicated via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an announcement after the class switch:  
  ```javascript
  function _onViewToggle(e) {
    …
    liveRegion.announce(`Product view switched to ${mode} view`); // ← add
  }
  ```

These twelve issue blocks satisfy the mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and cover additional WCAG‑related problems detected in the script.

fpTitles: Contact form fields do **not** set **aria‑invalid** on validation error

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance the dropdown panel is shown/hidden, yet the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute is never updated. Screen readers cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and after closing, setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (data‑period) are toggled with CSS classes only; no aria‑pressed attribute is set, so keyboard‑only users cannot perceive which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In _pricing._syncToggleState add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', isActive ? 'true' : 'false'); for each button.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑toggle switch missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (data‑billing‑toggle) changes visual state via CSS but never updates aria‑pressed, leaving its state ambiguous to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: In _pricing._onToggleClick or after setPeriod, call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', isYear ? 'true' : 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult adds error text and CSS classes but never adds aria-invalid="true" to the field when validation fails, violating ARIA error handling.
issuesFound.fix: In the if (!result.valid) branch, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and remove it in the valid branch.

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields missing aria‑invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form uses inlineValidator for its inputs; because the validator does not set aria-invalid, users of screen readers receive no programmatic error indication.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each bound field receives aria-invalid="true" when the validation result is invalid (see Issue 5).

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input missing aria‑invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form validates the email field via inlineValidator but the validator does not set aria-invalid, so error state is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same fix as Issue 5 to set aria-invalid="true" on the email input when invalid.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters updates the product count badge, empty‑state message, and pagination, but no aria‑live region is updated to announce these changes to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After applying filters, call liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${visibleCount} products`); or update a dedicated status element with aria-live="polite".

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod updates price displays and savings badges but does not provide an audible cue via a live region, leaving users unaware of the billing‑period change.
issuesFound.fix: After changing period, invoke liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);.

fpTitles: Contact form fields missing aria‑invalid on validation errors
Newsletter form email input missing aria‑invalid on validation errors

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle “Monthly” button missing aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle “Annual” button missing aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron span missing aria‑hidden

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements that do not have a more specific focus rule, blocking keyboard users from identifying their location.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and only adds outline-offset. Since outline width is effectively 0, the offset renders nothing, removing the focus ring for all buttons using this class.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link focus rule removes the outline without adding a box-shadow or border, making the link invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button loses its focus indicator because outline is set to none with no visible replacement, hindering keyboard access to the menu.

issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links have their focus outline removed without a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to track focus within the menu.

issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus rule removes the outline without providing a box-shadow or border, hiding focus state from keyboard users.

issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links lose their focus indicator due to outline: none without replacement, blocking keyboard access to account features.

issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the focus outline without replacement, making it difficult for keyboard users to identify the active filter.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible

issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes its focus outline without replacement, preventing keyboard users from knowing when the table region is focused for scrolling.

issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the focus outline without a visible alternative, blocking keyboard users from expanding questions.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes the button difficult to activate on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons require larger touch areas for accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle has min-height and min-width of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px size, making it hard to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has a fixed height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size, making it difficult to select on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28x28px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum for touch targets, rendering it nearly impossible to activate for some users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is sized at 20x20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, making it difficult to select on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes the content visually visible instead of hidden. This breaks the utility's purpose of hiding content visually while keeping it for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform and :active transform without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable the movement, which can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) to hide/show, which is spatial movement. There is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition and hover state. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 943 is empty and does not disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and significantly reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, providing no visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline. Keyboard users cannot perceive which element currently has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator for high-contrast mode users without providing a visible replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but lacks a visible alternative focus indicator. Users navigating by keyboard cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove the focus outline with outline: none and provide only outline-offset, leaving no visible indicator for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline with no visible replacement, preventing keyboard users from identifying the focused link.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. This creates a barrier for users who require larger touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px, meeting the minimum but falling below the recommended 44px touch target size without compensating padding or margin.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which keeps content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility and may cause layout issues while exposing visual content that should be hidden.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement on interaction, but there is no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties causing spatial movement, but the empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 877 fails to disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition, creating spatial movement on hover/focus, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to respect user motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image hover state applies transform properties for spatial movement without a corresponding reduced motion override, which may trigger vestibular disorders in sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover state uses transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) for spatial movement, but the existing prefers-reduced-motion media query only covers the base .pricing-card:hover selector, leaving this featured variant animation active for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The button active state uses transform: translateY(1px) for spatial movement on click, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this animation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below normal. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and violates WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The section header paragraph uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making it difficult for users with low vision to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger
issuesFound.explanation: The danger button uses background-color: var(--colour-error) which is #ee9999 with white text (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making the button label difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: The stat label uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% white opacity) against a gradient background of approximately #5588bb, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .pricing-toggle > span
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle label uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: The plan tagline uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: The input hint text uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer[role="contentinfo"]
issuesFound.explanation: The footer uses color: var(--colour-neutral-300) which is #cbd5e1 against a background of var(--colour-neutral-900) which is #555555, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand > p
issuesFound.explanation: The footer brand paragraph uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) which is #94a3b8 against a #555555 background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.0:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text (14px font size).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use color: var(--colour-neutral-400) which is #94a3b8 against a #555555 background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.0:1, failing WCAG AA for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: The footer bottom text uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) which is #64748b against a #555555 background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.8:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without additional non-color visual differentiators (such as border-bottom, font-weight changes, or background highlights) in the default state, links are distinguished from surrounding text only by color, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions omit aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions toggle the navigation drawer’s CSS classes but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the hamburger trigger button. Screen readers cannot convey whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, leaving blind users unaware of the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2374: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`
  Line 2393: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialization missing aria-expanded and aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, it does not set aria-expanded="false" to reflect the closed state on page load, nor does it set aria-controls to link the trigger button to the drawer panel. Assistive technologies cannot describe the relationship or current state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu registration omits aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menu pairs are registered, the trigger button does not receive an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute to indicate the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2580: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, insert:
  ```javascript
  if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
  ```

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox omits aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open() and _close() functions manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not toggle the aria-expanded attribute on the search input combobox. Screen readers will not announce when the suggestion dropdown becomes available or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3365: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;` in the _open function
  Line 3375: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;` in the _close function

issuesFound.title: siteSearch initialization missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch initialization does not set aria-expanded="false" on the search input element to reflect the closed state of the suggestion panel on initial page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: After `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period toggles omit aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) in the pricing module indicate active state via CSS classes but do not set the aria-pressed attribute. Screen readers cannot convey which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4236: Inside the `_periodBtns.forEach` loop in _syncToggleState, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, insert:
  ```javascript
  setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));
  ```

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause button omits aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle button in the testimonials carousel changes the auto-play state but does not update aria-pressed to reflect whether the carousel is currently playing (pressed) or paused (not pressed).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4480: In the play() function, after `_playing = true;`, insert `if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');`
  Line 4490: In the pause() function, after `_playing = false;`, insert `if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid omits aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1636: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function body.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid omits clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes error descriptions but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", leaving screen readers believing the field is still invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1648: After removing aria-describedby references, insert `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters omits live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but no aria-live announcement is made. Blind users are not informed how many products match the selected filters or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3716: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` inside applyFilters, insert:
  ```javascript
  liveRegion.announce(totalMatches === 0 ? STRINGS.filterNoResults || 'No products match your filters' : `${totalMatches} products found`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod omits live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually but no announcement is made to screen readers via the live region to confirm the billing period change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4182: At the end of the setPeriod function (before the closing brace), insert:
  ```javascript
  liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing' : STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing');
  ```

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer omits aria-hidden management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is shown and hidden via CSS classes, but the code does not set aria-hidden="true" when closed or remove it when open. Depending on CSS implementation, screen readers may still perceive the visually hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2374: In the open() function, after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, insert `if (_drawer) _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`
  Line 2393: In the close() function, after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, insert `if (_drawer) setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus management on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, and when it closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the document and cannot navigate the opened menu or return to their previous context.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initializes or activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard focus can escape the modal drawer and move to background elements, violating modal dialog accessibility requirements.

fpTitles: siteSearch initialization missing aria-expanded
mobileNav missing focus management on open/close

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by removing or setting the hidden attribute, but it never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current expanded state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2158: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _openInstance after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
    Line 2172: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _closeInstance after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) function as toggle buttons but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to indicate which period is currently active. Screen readers cannot convey the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2805: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the _periodBtns.forEach loop in _syncToggleState, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, it adds an error message and links it via aria-describedby, but it fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1403: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside markInvalid function after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked as valid, the function removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute, leaving it set to "true" incorrectly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1422: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` inside markValid function after removing aria-describedby references.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded on triggers
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls are set, but it does not initialise aria-expanded="false" for closed dropdowns on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2210: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside register function after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is discovered and event listeners attached, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" to indicate the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1915: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside init function after verifying _trigger and _drawer exist.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded updates on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input implements a combobox pattern but does not update aria-expanded when the suggestion list opens or closes, leaving screen readers unaware of the list visibility state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3120 (_open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 3130 (_close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion listbox is mounted, the input is given role="combobox" and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" for the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2525: Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside mount function after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for filter changes
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates and the grid changes, but no aria-live announcement is made to inform screen reader users how many products are now showing or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3445: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing {n} products').replace('{n}', String(totalMatches)));` inside the filterAnimation.run callback after _updateCountBadge.

issuesFound.title: pricing missing live region announcement for billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually with animation, but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2880: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after updating the toggle state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement for expand/collapse
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items expand and collapse with animation, but the state change is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users may not realize the content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3720 (open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title));`
    Line 3740 (close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but provides no auditory feedback via live regions for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2160 (_openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`
    Line 2175 (_closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the page layout significantly but does not announce the state change to screen readers.

    Line 1980 (close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: contactForm inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for personal data (first name, last name, email, company) do not have autocomplete attributes, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill and reducing accessibility for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email missing autocomplete attribute

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing initial aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher buttons missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons are discovered but their aria-pressed state is not initialised based on the current theme preference on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4540: Add `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current)); });` after setting the initial theme.

issuesFound.title: productFilter view toggle buttons missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The grid/list view toggle buttons do not have their aria-pressed attribute initialised to reflect the current view mode on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3340: Add `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, ARIA.PRESSED, String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode)); });` after restoring the saved view mode.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput missing aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the error message is displayed but aria-invalid is not set to "true" on the input field, so screen readers do not identify it as invalid.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField missing aria-invalid on file validation failure

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility using CSS classes but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded or collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1174: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Line 1189: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu control the visibility of dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button to reflect whether the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1284: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line 1293: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the beginning of the _closeInstance function before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open and _close functions display and hide the suggestion list panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input combobox to indicate whether the popup is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1511: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of the _open function.
  Line 1516: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of the _close function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute on the navigation toggle button. Since the menu starts closed, the trigger should have aria-expanded="false" so screen readers know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1244: After `_initialised = true;` and before event listeners, insert:
  `if (_trigger) { setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav')); }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init function registers dropdown triggers but does not set aria-expanded="false" on them during initialization, even though the panels start hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1361: Inside the forEach callback after `register(trigger, panel);`, insert `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function configures the search input as a combobox but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion panel is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1519: After `_initialised = true;`, insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the drawer by removing a CSS class, but does not set the hidden attribute or aria-hidden="true", leaving the closed drawer in the accessibility tree. Conversely, when opened, aria-hidden is not removed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1193: After `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, insert `_drawer.setAttribute('hidden', ''); setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
  Line 1178: After `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, insert `_drawer.removeAttribute('hidden'); setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product cards and the count badge, but does not announce the result count (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1827: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, insert `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.filterResultsCount || 'Showing {n} products').replace('{n}', String(totalMatches)));`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates price displays when switching between monthly and annual billing, but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1943: At the end of the setPeriod function (before the closing brace), insert `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices switched to {period} billing').replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the page context significantly, but no announcement is made to inform screen reader users that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1174: After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`, insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line 1189: After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`, insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing switch missing aria-checked updates
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle uses role="switch" when not a native button, but the code never sets or updates aria-checked to reflect whether the switch is in the "on" (annual) or "off" (monthly) state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1954: After `if (!_toggleEl.getAttribute('role')) _toggleEl.setAttribute('role', 'switch');`, insert `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');`
  Line 1967: In _onToggleClick, after `const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year';`, insert `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', next === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Carousel dots container missing tablist role
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonials carousel dots have role="tab" but are appended to a container (_dotsEl) that is not explicitly given role="tablist", which is required for the tab pattern to be complete.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080: After `_dotsEl = qs('[data-carousel-dots]', _rootEl);`, insert `if (_dotsEl) setAttr(_dotsEl, 'role', 'tablist');`

issuesFound.title: Wizard step panels misuse tabpanel role
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard applies role="tabpanel" to step panels, but the navigation uses "Previous/Next" buttons rather than tabs. This creates a mismatch with the ARIA tab pattern; the panels should use role="region" or no explicit role.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2281: Replace `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel');` with `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'region');` and remove `step.panelEl.setAttribute('aria-label', step.title);` (or keep it with role="region").

issuesFound.title: Focus trap references exports before initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The createFocusTrap function emits an event referencing 'exports' on line 1227, but the exports variable is not defined until line 1241. This causes a ReferenceError that prevents the focus trap from activating, breaking keyboard accessibility for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1227: Replace `{ trap: exports }` with `{ trap: { activate, deactivate, pause, resume, isActive } }` or move the `const exports = ...` line (1241) to appear before line 1227.

fpTitles: Dropdown triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
Carousel dots container missing tablist role
Wizard step panels misuse tabpanel role

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Scanning" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab button for the Scanning category visually indicates active state but lacks aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1173: Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Design" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab button for the Design category visually indicates active state but lacks aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1173: Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Testing" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab button for the Testing category visually indicates active state but lacks aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1173: Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Reporting" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab button for the Reporting category visually indicates active state but lacks aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1173: Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link applies a visual current indicator class (nav-link--current) when item.current is true but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is current in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 615: Add `aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}` to the anchor element props.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link applies a visual current indicator class (mobile-nav__link--current) when item.current is true but lacks aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line 723: Add `aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}` to the anchor element props.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop that renders a spinner and disables the button, but it does not communicate the loading state to assistive technology via aria-busy. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: Add `aria-busy={isLoading}` to the button element props.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard renders an article element containing pricing information but lacks aria-labelledby to associate it with the plan name heading (headingId). Screen reader users navigating by article landmarks cannot identify the specific plan card.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1285: Change `<article className="pricing-card__article">` to `<article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker (*) for required fields is not marked with aria-hidden, so screen readers may announce "star" separately from the "required" text that follows in the VisuallyHidden span, causing redundant or confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 414: Change ` *` to ` *`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button in the testimonials carousel is an IconButton containing only a chevron character (‹) without an aria-label. Screen reader users cannot identify this control's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1486: Add `aria-label="Previous testimonial"` to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button in the testimonials carousel is an IconButton containing only a chevron character (›) without an aria-label. Screen reader users cannot identify this control's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1491: Add `aria-label="Next testimonial"` to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a nav element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page (DesktopNav, MobileNav, Footer), screen reader users cannot distinguish this skip navigation from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 308: Change `<nav>` to `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a nav element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish the desktop navigation from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 595: Change `<nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav">` to `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Desktop" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection renders a section element without an aria-labelledby, despite having a visible h1 heading with id="hero-heading". Screen reader users navigating by landmark regions cannot identify this section.
issuesFound.fix: Line 878: Change `<section className="hero-section">` to `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: StatsBar region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar renders a section element without an aria-labelledby, despite having a visible h2 heading with id="stats-heading". Screen reader users navigating by landmark regions cannot identify this section.
issuesFound.fix: Line 922: Change `<section className="stats-bar">` to `<section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">`

fpTitles: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
StatsBar region missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button controls the visibility of the answer panel via aria-controls but does not communicate the expanded/collapsed state via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2320: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>`

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button manages the open/closed state of the mobile nav panel but lacks aria-expanded to communicate that state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1380: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop to provide an accessible name for the icon-only button, but none is provided. Screen readers will announce this as an unlabeled button.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1140: Add aria-label prop: `<IconButton aria-label="Close mobile navigation" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton that cycles through themes has no aria-label prop, violating the component's interface requirement and leaving the button without an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1260: Add aria-label prop using the LABELS mapping: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button is an IconButton without an aria-label prop, providing no accessible name to indicate its purpose to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2260: Add aria-label prop: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button is an IconButton without an aria-label prop, leaving it without an accessible name for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2270: Add aria-label prop: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references an error message ID 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but the FormGroup component is not provided the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element is never rendered with that ID. This creates a broken reference that points to a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2430: Pass errorId prop to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input conditionally references 'contact-name-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element is never rendered. This results in a broken ARIA reference when validation errors occur.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2580: Pass errorId prop to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'contact-email-error' via aria-describedby, but the FormGroup component lacks the errorId prop, preventing the ErrorMessage from rendering with the correct ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2600: Pass errorId prop to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'contact-subject-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not provided the errorId prop, so the error message element is never created.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2620: Pass errorId prop to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea references 'contact-message-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup does not receive the errorId prop, resulting in a missing target element for the reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2640: Pass errorId prop to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The button references statusId ('newsletter-status') via aria-describedby, but the ErrorMessage with that ID only renders when status === 'error'. When the form is idle or submitting, the reference points to a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2480: Conditionally add the attribute only when error exists: `aria-describedby={status === 'error' ? statusId : undefined}`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing button is part of a toggle group but lacks aria-pressed to indicate whether it is the currently selected option.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1980: Add aria-pressed: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button lacks aria-pressed to communicate its selected state in the billing period toggle group.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1990: Add aria-pressed: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button toggles a persistent state but does not communicate whether the video is currently playing or paused via aria-pressed. The changing aria-label helps, but aria-pressed is required for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1450: Add aria-pressed: `<Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link that represents the current page has a visual indicator (nav-link--current class) but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from identifying it as the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1040: Add aria-current attribute: `{item.label}`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link in the mobile menu has a visual current state indicator but no aria-current="page" attribute to communicate the current page to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1180: Add aria-current attribute: `{item.label}`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The section is a major page landmark but lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label to identify its purpose. The h1 heading inside provides visible context but is not programmatically associated with the section.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1420: Add aria-labelledby: `<section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">`

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section is a landmark region containing an sr-only h2 heading, but the section element lacks aria-labelledby to associate it with that heading, leaving the landmark unnamed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1520: Add aria-labelledby: `<section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">`

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card is an article element in a repeated grid but lacks aria-labelledby to reference its heading, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify which card they are reading.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2050: Add aria-labelledby: `<article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple nav elements (SkipLinks, DesktopNav, MobileNav, Footer), but the SkipLinks nav lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~652: Add aria-label: `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation landmark lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other nav elements on the page (SkipLinks, MobileNav, Footer navigation).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1000: Add aria-label: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Desktop navigation" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integrations for visual marquee looping is not marked with aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the integration list twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1620: Add aria-hidden: `<ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div used as interactive element without button role
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop element has an onClick handler to close the navigation but is rendered as a generic div. It is not focusable via keyboard and lacks the button role, making it inaccessible to keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1130: Add role, tabIndex, and keyboard handler: `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" aria-label="Close mobile navigation" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop that disables the button and shows a spinner, but it does not communicate the loading state to assistive technology via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~720: Add aria-busy: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The label element inside FormGroup receives an htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the label element. This breaks the programmatic association between the label and its input field, meaning screen reader users cannot identify which label belongs to which input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1028: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error states but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen reader users cannot perceive when the field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1048: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid to the element. Assistive technology users are not informed when the textarea content is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1063: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm TextInput aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has aria-describedby set to 'newsletter-email-error' when in error state, but FormGroup is not passed an errorId prop, so no element with that ID is rendered. This creates a broken reference that confuses screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1812: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name TextInput aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The name input references 'contact-name-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not provided the errorId prop, so the error message element lacks this ID. Screen readers cannot locate the error description.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1857: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email TextInput aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'contact-email-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup does not receive the errorId prop to render the error message with this ID, breaking the association.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1872: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject TextInput aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input uses aria-describedby pointing to 'contact-subject-error', but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop so no element has this ID. The error message is not programmatically linked.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1887: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message Textarea aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea references 'contact-message-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not passed errorId, so the ID does not exist in the DOM. Screen readers cannot announce the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1902: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name

issuesFound.fix: Line 1687: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands/collapses mobile navigation sections has aria-controls but no aria-expanded attribute. Assistive technology users cannot perceive the current state of the disclosure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1308: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger has aria-controls linking to the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the answer is currently visible or hidden.
