issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. Since the page contains multiple navigation landmarks (DesktopNav, MobileNav, FooterNav), screen reader users cannot distinguish this skip navigation from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1008: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation uses <nav> without an aria-label. Combined with other nav elements on the page, this creates multiple unlabelled navigation landmarks that screen reader users cannot differentiate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1248: <nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav" aria-label="Desktop navigation">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element inside the mobile panel lacks an aria-label. With multiple nav elements present, screen reader users cannot identify this as the mobile navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1298: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section uses a <section> element without aria-labelledby or aria-label. Although it contains a heading with id="hero-heading", the section is not programmatically associated with it, reducing landmark navigation effectiveness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1398: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar section contains a heading with id="stats-heading" but the section element lacks aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks encounter an unlabelled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1468: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card uses <article> without aria-labelledby, even though it contains a heading with a stable ID. Screen reader users cannot identify the specific plan when navigating by article landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1598: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button visually shows active state but lacks aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1568: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links marked with item.current receive a visual indicator class but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is current in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1260: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the button shows a spinner but lacks aria-busy. Screen reader users are not notified that the button is in a loading state and the action is in progress.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The second list of integration logos is a visual clone for seamless looping but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce the duplicate items, creating redundant noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1498: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field indicator (*) is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers announce "star" separately from the "(required)" text provided by VisuallyHidden, creating redundant or confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1032: *

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span inside the Button component is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may attempt to interpret the spinner element, while the loading state should be conveyed solely via the aria-busy attribute and visually hidden text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1090: <span aria-hidden="true" className="btn-spinner" />
